message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Implementing exif_transpose strip_metadata option; I need to edit ImageOps.py (exif_transpose function) and its tests. Let me know your feature and files so we can keep edits disjoint within ImageOps.py if needed.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: My feature: add transform_callback param to exif_transpose in ImageOps.py. I only need to touch exif_transpose function and its tests. If your feature also touches ImageOps.py, let's split by function/region so we don't collide.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: We both need to touch the same exif_transpose function in the same files, so true disjoint ownership isn't possible. Proposal: sequential editing to avoid clobbering. I'll go first: add my strip_metadata param + tests to exif_transpose, run the test suite, then send DONE. Once you get my DONE, pull the current file state and layer your transform_callback param on top (keep my code intact, just add your param/logic alongside). Then you send your own DONE. Reply ACCEPT if this ordering works for you, or propose the reverse order instead.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Our features both touch exif_transpose in the same file, so true disjoint ownership isn't possible. Proposal to minimize collision: I'll add a transform_callback keyword-only param placed after in_place in the signature, and only touch the 'method = {...}.get(orientation)' block (wrapping it: if transform_callback provided, use it instead of the dict lookup). I won't touch the exif-deletion/XMP-cleanup block below. Please keep your strip_metadata changes scoped to that later block (after 'method is not None') and avoid changing the method-lookup lines. If that works for you, ACCEPT and we'll each work independently in our own copies.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: ACCEPT your ordering: you go first (strip_metadata), send DONE when finished. I'll then layer transform_callback on top, keeping your code intact, and send my own DONE. Proceeding now to implement in my working copy.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Agreed: you own the signature/method-lookup block (lines with the orientation->method dict and transform_callback param); I'll scope my strip_metadata changes to the block after 'if method is not None:' (the exif-deletion/XMP-cleanup section) plus the elif/final return tail, and add my own param + docstring entry without touching your method-lookup lines. Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Confirming scope: to support 'sequence of methods' from the callback, I also need to touch the transpose-application lines (currently 'if in_place: image.im = image.im.transpose(method)...else: transposed_image = image.transpose(method)') to loop over multiple methods. I will NOT touch anything from 'exif_image = image if in_place else transposed_image' onward (exif/XMP cleanup, elif/return tail) - that stays exactly as-is for you. Implementing now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Finished strip_metadata implementation: added _strip_all_metadata() helper, strip_metadata kwarg on exif_transpose (overloads + main def + docstring), and calls in the method-is-not-None branch, the no-orientation/not-in-place branch, and the no-orientation/in_place branch. Only touched the exif-deletion/XMP block tail and the return statements, left your method-lookup block untouched. Tests added: test_exif_transpose_strip_metadata, test_exif_transpose_in_place_strip_metadata. Full Tests/test_imageops.py suite passes (36/36).

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Finished transform_callback implementation: added Callable import, transform_callback kwarg on exif_transpose (overloads + main def + docstring), and logic to use the callback's returned method/sequence-of-methods instead of the default orientation dict, plus looping transpose application to support sequences. Only touched the signature/method-lookup/transpose-application lines; left the exif-deletion/XMP-cleanup block and elif/return tail untouched. Tests added: test_exif_transpose_transform_callback. Full Tests/test_imageops.py suite passes (35/35) in my copy.
